title: Guidance on the regulatory enrolment process for medical devices: Regulatory enrolment process - Canada.ca
content:
## Guidance on the regulatory enrolment process for medical devices: Regulatory enrolment process
- Regulatory enrolment process
- Naming conventions, folder structure
- Transmission via CESG
- Important considerations
- Contacts, other resources
- Templates  and forms
- Company enrolment and amendment process
- Dossier  ID request process
- Application  process
REP uses XML files generated by web-based  templates for transmission to Health Canada, instead of using PDF forms. The following templates/forms are used to capture the necessary information related to company, dossier, transaction, and application  respectively:
- company (CO) template
- dossier ID request form for medical  devices
- regulatory transaction (RT) template
- application information (AI) template
Collectively, these templates and forms are used in 3 distinct  processes:
- company enrolment and amendment
- dossier identifier (ID) request
- filing a regulatory transaction (application)
These templates replace  the following forms:
- Class II, III and IV new licence  application forms
- Class II, III and IV licence  amendment forms
- all faxback (minor change)  forms and
- private label licence  application forms
The following  summary provides a high-level walk-through of the steps to consider  when you begin  to use the REP. The sections  with the detailed  requirements are specified  in each step. Be  sure to read the entire guidance thoroughly  before using the REP.
CESG account: All regulatory transactions using the REP must only be sent to Health  Canada via the CESG. If you do not have a CESG  account refer to the  guidance on filing  submissions electronically .
New company: If your company has never filed a regulatory transaction to Health  Canada for medical devices, then you will need to obtain a “company identifier (ID)” and contact identifiers (IDs) by using the REP company  (CO) template.
Refer to the section on company enrolment  and amendment process for information on the CO template and the process  for enrolling your company. Note that it can take up to 1 week to  obtain your company ID once you have provided all the necessary information.
Existing  company: If you have previously filed a medical  device regulatory transaction to Health Canada  and you do not know your company  ID, email the Medical  Devices Directorate.
Refer to the section on company enrolment  and amendment process for information on the CO template and the process for  amending your company information.
New dossier: If you are filing a regulatory transaction for a new dossier for  which a dossier identifier (ID) has not yet been assigned, you will need to submit a dossier ID request form.
Refer to the section on the dossier ID request process  for information on this form and its requirements. Note that it can take up  to 1 week to obtain your dossier ID once you have provided all the necessary  information.
Existing  dossier: If you have previously filed a regulatory  transaction to Health Canada for this particular dossier and you do not know your dossier ID, email  the Medical Devices  Directorate.
Filing  a regulatory transaction (application): Once your  CESG account is set up and you have your company IDs, contact IDs and dossier  ID, you are ready to file a regulatory transaction using the REP. Prepare your regulatory transaction  as per the regulatory requirements using ToC. However, instead of completing the PDF application forms, you must complete the REP regulatory transaction (RT) template and the application information (AI)  template (when required).
Refer to the section on application process  for information on the process  and conditions related  to the RT and AI  templates.
Refer to the section on application information  (AI) template and process for when the AI file should be included in the  regulatory transaction filed for review.
The RT and AI files should be included within  the regulatory transaction as instructed in the sections on naming conventions and folder structures.
Once your regulatory transaction is ready  for filing, refer to the section on transmission via CESG.
Note: Each regulatory transaction filed to Health  Canada for a REP dossier must include a RT file (for example, the initial transaction  for a regulatory activity, a transaction for the response to additional  information request).

## Company enrolment and amendment process
The company enrolment  process, using the CO template, is intended to ensure  that Health Canada has the  most up-to-date company and contact information at all times. It is the company’s responsibility to ensure that the information captured in the final CO XML file is updated  as required.
With REP,  the company enrolment and amendment  process can be completed at any time and is independent from the regulatory  transactions filed for review.

### New company enrolment
To complete the CO template, follow the  instructions outlined in this section. Once complete, click the “Submit  to Health Canada”  button to generate  an email template  with all the details auto-filled. Be sure to attach the draft CO  XML file from your local computer before sending. If the email  template is not automatically generated, follow the instructions under the “Submit  to Health Canada” button on the CO template  for email details.
After verification by Health Canada, the  company will receive the final CO XML file by email. The final CO file will contain  the company ID and contact  IDs, which should  be used to complete subsequent REP templates.

### Amend company enrolment
To amend company or contact information,  you must upload the latest final company XML file received from Health Canada and click  the “Amend enrolment” button.
- If company representative details  have changed, update  the contact details  and click the “Revise contact” button.
- If a contact is no longer  active, click the “Delete contact”  button.
- To add a new company representative, click the “Add contact”  and “Save contact” buttons after completing all of the required fields.
The amendment process  is repeated each time the information on the latest CO file needs updating. Failure to use the most recent  final CO XML for amendments will result in delays.
Companies that know their company ID but do  not have their final company XML file can request a copy of their CO. To do so,  send an email to devicelicensing-homologationinstruments@hc-sc.gc.ca with the following information:
- Subject line: MD REP – CO request  – <Insert Company  ID here>
- MD REP – CO request  – <Insert Company  ID here>
- Body: company name company ID medical device licence numbers (if  applicable) contact information where CO should  be sent
- medical device licence numbers (if  applicable)
- contact information where CO should  be sent
Note: This contact must have had previous correspondence with Health Canada as a member of the  company in question

### Instructions for the CO template  and the enrolment process
- Access the online template  from the REP information page.
- Enter the information in the required  fields. Instructions can be found by clicking  the numbered hyperlink on the  right side of specific sections or fields.
- If a template is partially completed, click on the “Save working  copy” button at the bottom  of the template to save a copy  on the local workstation for completion later. The file will be saved in the  “.hcsc” file format. The working copies (files with extension .hcsc) should  never be sent to Health Canada. To resume  completing the template, the working copy file should  be uploaded back into a blank CO template using  the “Choose file ” button near the top of the template.
- Once the template is completed with all the required fields,  click the “Save draft XML” button near the bottom  of the template to save the XML file on your local workstation.
- If there are errors, the XML will not be  generated. The errors will be presented near the top of the template. A specific error may be  selected to go directly to the section or field that needs to be corrected.
- Once the draft CO XML file is generated, it is ready to be sent to Health Canada.
- Click on the “Submit to Health  Canada” button near the bottom of template to create an email with the email address and subject pre-filled using your local email client  application. Attach the draft  CO XML file to the email before sending to Health Canada. If your email client  is not configured on your computer or the “Submit  to Health Canada” button does not automatically  launch your default email client application, follow the instructions at the  bottom of the CO template.
- If your email client  is not configured on your computer or the “Submit  to Health Canada” button does not automatically  launch your default email client application, follow the instructions at the  bottom of the CO template.

### Instructions for amending  a final CO XML file
- Load the most recent final CO file (received  from Health Canada)  into a blank CO template  using the “Choose file ” button near the top of the template.
- Select the “Amend enrolment” button before making any changes.
- Make the necessary revisions.
- Follow steps 2 to 7 in the previous section on instructions for CO template  and enrolment process.
Health Canada may request clarification by  email by responding to the sender that filed the draft CO XML file. After verification, Health  Canada will update the internal database and assign a new company ID, if applicable. Health Canada will return the final  CO file by email by responding to the sender that filed the draft CO XML file.  The final CO file will contain the company ID, which will be required to  complete the dossier ID request  form and the RT and AI templates. Company  ID is also indicated as the  5-digit number within the CO XML file name (for example, final-com-K 18276 -2022-03-16-1204.xml).
This process can take up to 5 business  days. If you require a status update and the processing of your company  enrolment and amendment has been more than 5 business days, email Health Canada at devicelicensing-homologationinstruments@hc-sc.gc.ca. To ensure that this  does not cause delays with any forthcoming regulatory transactions, the company  enrolment and amendment should be completed as early as possible.
Note the  following conditions:
- Each company that interacts  with Health Canada must enroll their company (for example, manufacturer, invoicing company,  regulatory correspondent). Once the company  is enrolled in REP,  all company information updates must be provided using the CO template for all REP and non-REP  dossiers. If the regulatory correspondent  for questions related to specific regulatory activities or medical device  licences is a third party, they must enrol their own company for a separate CO  xml.
- The company ID and contact IDs  obtained via enrolment should be shared with the manufacturer or regulatory correspondent, so that they can reference the appropriate IDs when  completing the RT template.
- Companies should allow a  minimum of 7 business days for the company enrolment and amendment process.  However, to ensure that this does not delay any forthcoming regulatory transactions, companies are encouraged to initiate a company  enrolment before filing a regulatory transaction to Health Canada.
- If there are changes  to the company name, company address, manufacturing facility  address or a transfer of licences from another manufacturer, all  impacted licences should be identified on the CO file. Upon Health Canada’s  verification, a final CO XML file and updated copies of all impacted  licences will be returned to the manufacturer (replacement of the current  manufacturer name or address change faxback process).
- All supporting documents that are required  to support any of these changes should  be submitted to Health Canada along with the draft CO XML file.
- If licence ownership is being transferred, follow the details  on the CO template and provide all necessary details as instructed to  prevent delays.
- If your company information has changed (for example, buyouts, mergers, name change) and a  regulatory transaction will need to be filed, be sure to complete the company  enrolment and amendment process before  filing the regulatory transaction. This is because a new company ID may need to  be assigned.
Figure 1: Overview of company enrolment and amendment process
The following process map shows the key  steps in enrolling or amending a company profile with Health Canada.
- Do you  have a final CO XML file? If yes,  proceed to step 2. If no,  proceed to step 4.
- If yes,  proceed to step 2.
- If no,  proceed to step 4.
- Is your  final CO XML file up to date? If yes, proceed  with REP process using your Company ID. If no,  proceed to step 3.
- If yes, proceed  with REP process using your Company ID.
- If no,  proceed to step 3.
- Amend your  final CO XML file by uploading it into the blank CO template and selecting the  “Amend enrolment” button, making the required modifications. Proceed to step 5.
- Complete a  new CO template to enrol your company.
- Submit the  draft CO XML generated by the CO template to Health Canada via email.
- Health  Canada will verify the information submitted on the draft CO XML and populate  internal systems accordingly. A final CO XML will be generated by Health Canada  and issued to the company via email.

## Dossier ID request process
A dossier is a collection of all regulatory  activities throughout the lifecycle of a licence. The dossier identifier (ID) is assigned  by Health Canada  to uniquely identify  the dossier. It consists  of a lowercase letter “m”, followed by 6 numeric digits.
The purpose of the dossier ID request  process is for manufacturers to obtain an identifier for a dossier by submitting a completed dossier  ID request form to Health  Canada. Once Health Canada has received  all the essential information, a unique dossier ID will be assigned. The  manufacturer will require this before completing the REP templates for the  regulatory transaction filing process and for creating the top-level folder  name. If the wrong dossier ID is used, delays may occur or your transaction may be rejected.

### When to request  a dossier ID
Completing  a dossier ID request form for a regulatory transaction that you intend to file is only required if the transaction is for a new dossier (a dossier ID has not yet been assigned).
Manufacturers that hold an active medical  device licence are able to request a complete  listing of their existing dossier IDs by emailing us at devicelicensing- homologationinstruments@hc-sc.gc.ca with the following information:
- Subject line: MD REP – DO request
- Body: company name company ID medical device licence numbers  (if applicable) contact information where DO should be  sent
- medical device licence numbers  (if applicable)
- contact information where DO should be  sent
Note: This contact must have had previous correspondence with Health Canada as a member of the  company in question.

### Instructions for dossier  ID request form and process
The dossier ID request form:
- is accessible by clicking on  the appropriate form link on the REP information page . Be sure to select  the correct link as the completed form will be submitted to a different Health Canada group depending on  the dossier type.
- Be sure to select  the correct link as the completed form will be submitted to a different Health Canada group depending on  the dossier type.
- generates an automatic email  sent to Health Canada once it has been completed and the “Submit”  button is clicked
- does not generate an XML file
- should be completed as per the instructions provided  on the “Help text” section  of the form
- will prompt a thank you page  if submitted successfully The primary contact should  receive a copy of the completed form at the email  address listed in the form. If after clicking the “Submit”  button you do not receive a thank you page or the primary contact does not  receive a copy of the completed form by email, contact us at devicelicensing-homologationinstruments@hc- sc.gc.ca to  ensure we received your request.
- The primary contact should  receive a copy of the completed form at the email  address listed in the form.
- If after clicking the “Submit”  button you do not receive a thank you page or the primary contact does not  receive a copy of the completed form by email, contact us at devicelicensing-homologationinstruments@hc- sc.gc.ca to  ensure we received your request.
An email will be sent to  the primary contact if more information is required. Otherwise, a dossier ID will be assigned and sent to the  primary contact within a week from the request.
Note the  following conditions:
- Companies are expected to  include any relevant information, including any related licences and previous  transactions, in the “Additional field for context” to assist with the review of the request.
- Companies should allow a  minimum of 5 business days for the dossier request process. However, to ensure that this does not cause delays with any forthcoming regulatory transactions, companies may initiate a dossier  request before filing a regulatory transaction to Health Canada.
- If Health Canada requires  more information, the primary  and alternate contacts will be contacted by email.
Figure  2: Overview of the dossier  request process
The following process map shows the key  steps in requesting a new Dossier ID which will be required for filing a  subsequent regulatory transaction such as a new medical device licence  application.
- Do you  have an up-to-date final CO XML file associated with this dossier? If yes,  proceed to step 3. If no,  proceed to step 2.
- If yes,  proceed to step 3.
- If no,  proceed to step 2.
- Enrol your  company or amend your final CO XML through the company enrolment process.
- Do you  have a dossier ID? If yes,  proceed with REP using your existing dossier ID. If no,  proceed to step 4 to request a dossier ID.
- If yes,  proceed with REP using your existing dossier ID.
- If no,  proceed to step 4 to request a dossier ID.
- Complete and  submit a new dossier ID request form to Health Canada.
- Health  Canada will validate the information submitted on the dossier ID request form  and populate internal systems accordingly. A dossier ID will be generated by  Health Canada and issued to the company via email.
The application process  pertains to the remaining 2 REP templates:
- regulatory transaction (RT) template and
- application information (AI)  template
Using these templates, final REP XML files  can be generated by the company when needed and do not need to be finalized by  Health Canada in advance. These files should be included within a regulatory  transaction to be filed to Health Canada.
A regulatory transaction containing final  REP XML files must not include any of the following forms:
- Class II, III or IV new licence  application form
- licence amendment application  form
- minor change (faxback) form and
- private label licence  application form
The information from these  forms is superseded by the information in the REP XML  files. If these forms are  filed in a regulatory transaction along  with a REP XML file, the transaction  may be rejected.
Refer to Figure  4 for the placement of the final RT and AI XML files in the application folder structure.
The device details spreadsheet is required  for all new licence applications and any amendment applications that may impact  a manufacturer’s catalogue  listing. A signed attestation form must also be completed for the  initial transaction of a new  regulatory activity.
Refer to Figure 4 for the placement of the device  details spreadsheet and the signed  attestation form in the application folder structure.
Files should not have  any additional security settings. For example, a file:
- should not  have password protection, restricted access, Digital Rights Management (DRM) or  Information Rights Management (IRM) that prevents it from opening Files with  these settings may be blocked by our organization’s security policies,  preventing us from accessing them.
- Files with  these settings may be blocked by our organization’s security policies,  preventing us from accessing them.
- should be  set to allow printing, selecting text and graphics, and adding or changing  notes and form fields

### Instructions for RT and AI templates and the application process
- Access the online templates  from the REP information page .
- Enter the information in the required  fields. Instructions can be found by clicking  the numbered hyperlink of  specific sections or fields. The fields on the templates are  built interdependently, meaning various picklists are tailored to the selections you make in previous fields. Be  sure to select the correct items from the picklists to  avoid delays in the processing of your transaction.
- The fields on the templates are  built interdependently, meaning various picklists are tailored to the selections you make in previous fields.
- Be  sure to select the correct items from the picklists to  avoid delays in the processing of your transaction.
- If a template is partly  completed, click the “Save working copy” button at the bottom of the template to save a copy on the local workstation for completion  later. The file will be saved in the “.hcsc” file format. The working copies (files with  extension .hcsc) should never be sent to Health  Canada. To resume completing the template, the working copy can be loaded back into a blank RT or AI template using  the “Choose file” button near the top of the template.
- The file will be saved in the “.hcsc” file format.
- The working copies (files with  extension .hcsc) should never be sent to Health  Canada.
- To resume completing the template, the working copy can be loaded back into a blank RT or AI template using  the “Choose file” button near the top of the template.
- Once the template is completed  with all the required fields, click the “Generate Final XML” button found near the bottom of the template  to save the XML file on the local workstation. Any errors will be presented near the top of the template. A specific error may be selected to go directly to  the section or field that needs to be corrected.
- Any errors will be presented near the top of the template. A specific error may be selected to go directly to  the section or field that needs to be corrected.
- Once the final XML file is generated, it must be sent to Health Canada  within a regulatory transaction submitted for review. Refer to the sections on folder structure and naming conventions, and transmission via the  CESG for detailed instructions.
- Refer to the sections on folder structure and naming conventions, and transmission via the  CESG for detailed instructions.
- To pre-populate a blank template  using a previously generated XML file, upload the file into the  corresponding blank template (for example, the RT file into the RT template)  using the “Choose file ” button near  the top of the template. Revise the information as needed.

### Regulatory transaction (RT) template and process
The regulatory transaction template collects information pertaining to the:
- regulatory activity  and transaction (for example, application number, regulatory activity type, transaction description) and
- regulatory contact  identifier for this regulatory activity or transaction
Note the  following conditions:
- Once a company files a  regulatory transaction containing a final RT file, that dossier is considered an REP dossier.  All subsequent regulatory transactions must also contain the required  REP files as transactions missing a correct REP file will be rejected.
- When viewing a final RT file,  upload the file into a blank RT template.
- A new or revised RT file must  be created for each regulatory transaction containing the information pertaining to that transaction. Do not submit a previously filed RT XML file  without updating the required information.
- When creating a final RT file  for a subsequent regulatory transaction, upload a recent final RT file into a blank RT template  to prepopulate all the fields.  Note it is the company’s responsibility to ensure the pre-populated information is  accurate.
The following guidelines can help complete  the RT template:
- A previously submitted RT file may be uploaded  into a blank template to pre-populate the fields. Be sure to revise all the  required fields pertaining to the regulatory transaction you are currently  filing. The pre-populated information must be accurate and applicable before an updated  RT file is generated. The information in the RT must align with  the information in the cover letter. If conflicting information is  provided, processing delays will occur as Health Canada needs to confirm the  intent to accurately process the transaction.
- Be sure to revise all the  required fields pertaining to the regulatory transaction you are currently  filing.
- The pre-populated information must be accurate and applicable before an updated  RT file is generated. The information in the RT must align with  the information in the cover letter.
- If conflicting information is  provided, processing delays will occur as Health Canada needs to confirm the  intent to accurately process the transaction.
- Select the items in each  picklist field (especially regulatory activity types, and transaction description)  carefully. Your selection in a given field  will, in most cases, determine the picklists displayed  in other subsequent fields. Incorrect selections could delay the processing  of the transaction or possible improper routing of the transaction.
- Your selection in a given field  will, in most cases, determine the picklists displayed  in other subsequent fields.
- Incorrect selections could delay the processing  of the transaction or possible improper routing of the transaction.
- The designated regulatory  contact provided on the RT template is the individual that will be contacted for regulatory questions  related to that regulatory activity. A contact may be updated as part of a subsequent  transaction (for example, response to clarification request). It is important keep this  information up to date. Updates to the regulatory  activity may be filed as a subsequent transaction containing an updated RT file  referencing the correct application number with the new contact information Updates should be filed as  “unsolicited information” (as the transaction description).
- It is important keep this  information up to date.
- Updates to the regulatory  activity may be filed as a subsequent transaction containing an updated RT file  referencing the correct application number with the new contact information
- Updates should be filed as  “unsolicited information” (as the transaction description).
Figure  3: Overview of the regulatory transaction template process
The following process map shows the key  steps required for completing the regulatory transaction template process. This  process collects information such as the regulatory activity being performed as  well a description of the transaction. Steps
- Do you  have a valid company ID and is all company and contact information with Health  Canada up-to-date? If yes,  proceed to step 3. If no,  proceed to step 2.
- If yes,  proceed to step 3.
- If no,  proceed to step 2.
- Enrol your  company or amend your final CO XML through the company enrolment process.
- Do you  have a dossier ID? If yes,  proceed to step 5. If no,  proceed to step 4.
- If yes,  proceed to step 5.
- If no,  proceed to step 4.
- Request a  dossier ID through the dossier ID request process.
- Complete  the RT template for a new or existing regulatory activity. Once completed  generate a final RT XML file.

### Application information (AI) template and process
The AI template collects information  related to the application and its corresponding devices. To complete the AI template,  follow the steps outlined in the  section on the application process and include it within the regulatory  transaction.
The following conditions must be noted:
- The final AI file is not required in every transaction filed to Health  Canada.
- A final AI file must be included within  the initial transaction for the following  regulatory activities: new licence application (Class  II, III, IV) licence amendment application (Class  II, III, IV) private label licence application (Class II, III, IV) private label licence amendment application (Class II, III, IV)
- new licence application (Class  II, III, IV)
- licence amendment application (Class  II, III, IV)
- private label licence application (Class II, III, IV)
- private label licence amendment application (Class II, III, IV)
- An updated final AI file must be included  in the next transaction for a regulatory activity only if: information provided  in the most recent final AI file for that regulatory activity  has changed or Health Canada has requested an updated final AI file in the form of a request  for clarification. Note that if the final AI file is missing,  the regulatory transaction may be rejected.
- information provided  in the most recent final AI file for that regulatory activity  has changed or
- Health Canada has requested an updated final AI file in the form of a request  for clarification.
- Note that if the final AI file is missing,  the regulatory transaction may be rejected.
- When updating a final AI file, load the file into a blank AI template  and revise the relevant fields.
- It is the company’s responsibility to ensure  the pre-populated information is accurate.
